Maximal pattern mining only finds patterns with a minimum support threshold, while closed pattern mining finds all patterns.

Data mining is the process of extracting useful patterns and knowledge from large datasets.
Data preprocessing is an optional step in the data mining process.
Classification is a data mining technique used for predicting continuous numerical values.
Data mining can be used to improve business operations and decision-making.
Data cleaning is the process of transforming raw data into a format suitable for analysis.
Data integration is the process of combining data from different sources into a single dataset.
Discretization is the process of transforming continuous data into discrete values.
Data reduction techniques are used to eliminate noisy or irrelevant data from a dataset.
The Euclidean distance is a measure of similarity between two data points.
Cosine similarity is commonly used in text mining to measure the similarity between documents.
The Jaccard similarity coefficient is used to measure the similarity between continuous numerical values.
The Manhattan distance is equivalent to the L1 norm.
Association rule mining is used to identify patterns in transactional data.
The Apriori algorithm is used to identify frequent itemsets in a dataset.
Support measures the frequency of an itemset in the dataset.
The confidence of an association rule measures how often the antecedent and consequent items appear together in the dataset.
One of the key challenges in data mining is dealing with the curse of dimensionality.
The goal of data cleaning is to eliminate all noisy data from the dataset.
Clustering is a type of supervised learning algorithm.
Data preprocessing is not a necessary step in data mining, and can be skipped if the dataset is already clean and well-organized.
The goal of data transformation is to convert data from one format to another, such as from numerical to categorical data.
Data discretization is a technique used to transform continuous data into discrete data by dividing the data into intervals or bins.
Data normalization is a technique used to transform data so that it has a mean of zero and a standard deviation of one.
Data reduction techniques are used to remove irrelevant or redundant data from the dataset in order to reduce its size and complexity.
Euclidean distance is a commonly used distance metric in data mining that is calculated as the sum of the absolute differences between the values of the corresponding attributes.
Manhattan distance is a distance metric that calculates the distance between two points as the sum of the absolute differences between the values of the corresponding attributes.
The cosine similarity measure is a distance metric that measures the angle between two vectors in a high-dimensional space.
A dendrogram is a tree-like diagram used to represent the results of hierarchical clustering.
Association rule mining is a data mining technique used to discover patterns of association between different items in a dataset.
The support of an itemset is the percentage of transactions in the dataset that contain that itemset.
The confidence of a rule is the percentage of transactions that contain the antecedent of the rule that also contain the consequent.
The Apriori algorithm is a bottom-up approach to association rule mining that starts with frequent itemsets and builds larger itemsets by combining them.
In frequent pattern mining, a pattern is considered frequent if it occurs in at least a certain percentage of transactions in the dataset.
Decision trees can be used for both classification and regression analysis.
Data preprocessing involves the cleaning and transformation of data.
Association rule mining is used to identify frequent patterns in data.
Neural networks are a type of unsupervised learning algorithm.
Clustering is a supervised learning technique.
Data reduction techniques involve compressing and summarizing large amounts of data.
The K-nearest neighbors algorithm is an example of a parametric method.
The Apriori algorithm is used to mine association rules in large datasets.
The mean is a measure of central tendency used in data analysis.
Feature scaling is the process of converting data to a uniform scale.
Generating association rules from frequent itemset is the difficult part of mining operation.
